scenarios.name: Employment Termination Risk Assessment
scenarios.description: Review a proposed employee termination for legal risks before execution
scenarios.clientPrompt: We need to terminate Sarah Mitchell, a Senior Product Manager who has been with us for 7 years. She's been on a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) for 3 months. However, she recently filed an internal complaint about her manager's "hostile behavior" and also mentioned she's 4 months pregnant during a team meeting last week. HR wants to proceed with termination based on the PIP failure. What are the legal risks and how should we proceed?

employee.name: Sarah Mitchell
employee.title: Senior Product Manager
employee.pipReason: Missed product deadlines, team communication issues
recentEvents: Filed internal complaint about manager 2 weeks ago
PIP review scheduled for next week

learningObjectives: Identify protected class issues in termination decisions

scenarios.name: Enterprise SaaS Contract Review
scenarios.description: Review and negotiate a major enterprise software contract with significant liability exposure
scenarios.clientPrompt: We're about to sign a 3-year, $2.4M enterprise contract with CloudMatrix for their AI analytics platform. Their contract includes: (1) Unlimited liability for us if we breach their acceptable use policy, (2) They can change pricing with 30 days notice, (3) No SLA guarantees, just 'commercially reasonable efforts', (4) They own all derivative works from our data, (5) Mandatory arbitration in Delaware. Our CEO wants to sign by Friday. What should we push back on?

context.businessCriticality: Platform will process all customer data

learningObjectives: Balance business urgency with legal protection

scenarios.name: SEC Inquiry Response Strategy
scenarios.description: Develop response strategy for an SEC informal inquiry about executive stock sales
scenarios.clientPrompt: Our General Counsel just received an informal inquiry letter from the SEC. They're asking about stock sales by our CFO in the 2 weeks before we announced disappointing Q3 earnings. The CFO sold $1.2M in stock through a 10b5-1 plan that was established 4 months ago. However, the plan was modified 6 weeks before the sales to increase the number of shares. The stock dropped 34% after the earnings announcement. How do we respond?

context.inquiry: SEC Division of Enforcement - Informal Inquiry
context.timingIssue: Sales occurred 2 weeks before negative earnings

scenarios.name: M&A Due Diligence - Red Flag Analysis
scenarios.description: Analyze due diligence findings and advise on deal structure for a problematic acquisition target
scenarios.clientPrompt: We're acquiring FinServ Solutions for $85M. Due diligence revealed: (1) 3 pending class actions for data breaches totaling $40M in claimed damages, (2) Their main product may infringe a competitor's patent - they received a cease & desist 6 months ago, (3) 2 whistleblower complaints to the DOL about overtime violations, (4) The founder/CEO has a non-compete from his previous employer that arguably covers this business, (5) $12M in revenue comes from one customer whose contract expires in 4 months. Should we walk away or can we structure around these issues?

context.dealType: Asset purchase vs. Stock purchase TBD

redFlags.concentration: $12M revenue from single customer expiring soon

context.strategicValue: Would give us 40% market share in payments vertical

keyIssues: Successor liability in asset vs. stock deal
learningObjectives: Balance strategic value against legal risk

scenarios.name: Mock Trial - Wrongful Termination
scenarios.description: Full mock trial simulation with judge, jury, and attorneys for a wrongful termination case
scenarios.clientPrompt: Run a mock trial for Martinez v. TechCorp Industries. Plaintiff Maria Martinez claims she was fired in retaliation for reporting sexual harassment by her supervisor. TechCorp claims she was terminated for poor performance documented over 6 months. Key evidence: (1) Martinez's HR complaint filed 3 weeks before termination, (2) Performance reviews showing 'meets expectations' for 4 years, then suddenly 'needs improvement' after complaint, (3) Text messages from supervisor saying 'she'll regret this', (4) TechCorp's policy requiring progressive discipline that wasn't followed. Damages sought: $2.1M. Simulate opening statements, key witness examination, closing arguments, and jury deliberation.

context.venue: Superior Court of California, Santa Clara County

plaintiff: HR complaint filed 3 weeks before termination

learningObjectives: Evaluate evidence strength from both sides

quickPrompts.employmentTermination: We need to terminate an employee who recently filed a discrimination complaint. They've been on a PIP for 2 months. What are the risks?
quickPrompts.contractReview: Review this vendor contract for red flags. They want unlimited liability from us and can change pricing anytime.
quickPrompts.regulatoryInquiry: We received an SEC inquiry about insider trading. Our CFO sold stock 2 weeks before bad earnings. How do we respond?
quickPrompts.maRedFlags: Due diligence found 3 lawsuits and a patent issue. Is this deal still worth doing?
quickPrompts.mockTrial: Run a mock trial for a wrongful termination retaliation case with full jury deliberation.
